task.title: Persist ordered session conversation turns
task.objective: Complete issue https://github.com/Knosence9/project-vela/issues/495 by persisting user-visible human and assistant turns in the existing session aggregate without inventing system, provider, tool, permission, or extensible actor semantics.
acceptance_criteria: Open sessions append non-empty human and assistant turns as payload-version-1 events and replay exact content in persisted order.
Closing preserves prior turns and rejects new ones without writing, while reopening permits later turns in the same transcript.
Missing sessions, malformed turn payloads, unsupported versions, and invalid lifecycle ordering remain explicit errors.
Concurrent valid turn appends both persist in one valid order, and load and list expose the same transcript after reopening the database.
Documentation, independent review, local verification, required hosted checks, and squash merge complete successfully.

attempts.summary: Resolved the architecture tracker with the approved smallest conversation contract and created bounded issue 495.
attempts.diagnostic: The selected boundary used a closed Human/Assistant role enum, opaque non-empty content, the existing session stream, and open-session-only appends; system prompts, provider/model metadata, tool traffic, permissions, and extensible actor identity remained explicit non-goals.
attempts.patch: Closed decision issue 494 with the approved contract before changing persisted behavior.

attempts.summary: Wrote focused conversation acceptance tests before production support.
attempts.diagnostic: The RED suite failed to compile because SessionTurnRole, SessionTurnContent, SessionTurnContentError, SessionStore::append_turn, Session::turns, and SessionStoreError::SessionClosed did not exist.
attempts.patch: Added scenarios for ordered replay, exact payloads, close and reopen interaction, missing and closed sessions, concurrent appends, malformed role and content payloads, unsupported versions, and invalid history.

attempts.summary: Implemented ordered conversation persistence in the existing session aggregate.
attempts.diagnostic: The focused GREEN suite passed 4 tests, and the repeated repository gate passed 121 tests with no formatting, checking, Clippy, SecretSpec, or diff failures.
attempts.patch: Added validated turn types, session.turn_appended encoding and decoding, open-session append with optimistic retry, transcript projection through load and list, explicit closed-session rejection, and observable-contract documentation.

attempts.summary: Completed independent pre-commit review and hosted verification.
attempts.diagnostic: Pinned Codex review found no blocking correctness issues. Pull request 496 passed required CodeRabbit and Rust quality statuses; CodeRabbit reported a temporary review-volume limit in its summary, so no unobserved detailed findings are claimed.
attempts.patch: Kept the reviewed implementation unchanged except for documentation precision, a list-projection assertion, and an accurate internal unreachable diagnostic, then reran the full local gate.

attempts.summary: Squash-merged and synchronized the conversation capability.
attempts.diagnostic: Pull request 496 merged as b6ff1739b24c633f72eeb7a0fdb5a59caf2085dc after Rust quality gate run 29465752396 and the required CodeRabbit status passed; local main fast-forwarded to the merge commit.
attempts.patch: Linked issue 495, enabled squash auto-merge, monitored required statuses, and synchronized the local repository before recording this episode.

outcome.summary: Vela sessions now durably preserve an ordered user-visible human and assistant transcript across close, reopen, database reopen, and deterministic listing while rejecting closed-session writes and corrupt histories explicitly.

verification.command: nix develop --command cargo test --locked -p vela-kernel --test session_conversation # RED: compile failure because the turn contract did not exist
nix develop --command cargo test --locked -p vela-kernel --test session_conversation # GREEN: 4 passed, 0 failed
nix develop --command codex review --uncommitted # no blocking correctness findings
nix develop --command just verify # formatting, workspace checks, 121 tests, Clippy, SecretSpec, and diff gates passed
GitHub Rust quality gate run 29465752396 and required CodeRabbit status for pull request 496

lessons: A bounded transcript can use the session event stream for both lifecycle and turn ordering without introducing a second index or migration.
A closed Human/Assistant role set records user-visible conversation while deliberately postponing system, model, tool, permission, and personality semantics.
Open-session-only append rules should be enforced both at command time and during history replay so races and corrupt stored orderings fail consistently.
Optimistic retry lets concurrent valid turns both persist while a competing close causes the retried writer to observe and respect the closed state.
Collection queries should project conversation fields through the same aggregate projector as load-by-identifier to prevent transcript drift.
Hosted status success and review-comment content are distinct evidence; a rate-limited review summary should be reported transparently rather than described as a detailed review.
